$comment: Contract between demo-two-protocols.html and the demo.honua.io seeding task. Endpoint ids reuse the maui-zoning entries already pinned in assets/demo/layers.json (FeatureServer path, OGC API Features collection id). ADDITIONAL contract this page introduces: (1) the zoning layer's attribute schema publishes a string field zone_code (numeric-string codes like '030'='R-3 Residential', '500'='AG Agriculture'); (2) the layer is queryable through GeoServices REST (where=), OGC API Features (filter= cql2-text), and OData v4 (/odata/Layers(2)/Features?$filter=) so all three lanes return identical feature sets; (3) CORS per layers.json applies to /odata/* as well. Updated 2026-06-12: layerId 0→2, filterField ZONE_CLASS→zone_code, filterOptions updated to real server codes.

layer.title: Zoning — Maui, Hawaiʻi
layer.attribution: County of Maui · Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS Program

odata.$comment: Honua Server's OData surface keys layers by a server-wide integer (/odata/Layers({id})/Features). The live lane resolves that id at runtime by matching Name in GET /odata/Layers against these hints; the fixture lane uses the placeholder id baked into fixtures/odata.json. Real OData field names for maui-zoning (Id=2): zone_code, zone_dist, cp_area, island (no ACRES on this layer).
